title: The 1973 Arab-Israeli War: The Albatross of Decisive Victory
author_or_org: George W. Gawrych / Combat Studies Institute, U.S. Army Command and General Staff College
source_type: scholarly military history / official military-study monograph
strengths: Page-specific opening-force counts and an operational chronology useful for phase boundaries and denominator reconstruction.
limitations: Civilian mortality is largely absent; casualty reporting is selective and uneven by phase.
notes: Core source for Sinai opening denominators and the Egyptian early-death floor.

title: The Saga of OZ 77 in the Arab-Israeli War of 1973: A Small Armor Unit's Fight in a Large-Scale Combat Operation
strengths: Focused reconstruction of the decisive opening Golan fight with a clear four-day window and Israeli opening-force figure.
limitations: Not a casualty compendium; concentrated on one battle and tactical experience.
notes: Best single source for treating 6-9 October on the Golan as a separate episode.

title: No Victor, No Vanquished: The Yom Kippur War
variables_supported: civilian deaths and injuries in Port Said
strengths: Contains several theater-specific casualty statements, including one of the clearest civilian signals (Port Said).
limitations: Mixes official admissions, claims, and later reporting; some whole-war Arab death figures are explicitly conflicting and should not be averaged mechanically.
notes: Used selectively for front/window claims; whole-war death figures on p.154 are treated as conflicting context, not direct input.

title: The Yom Kippur War: The Epic Encounter That Transformed the Middle East
strengths: Useful cross-check on chronology, opening intensity, and whole-war Israeli losses.
limitations: Not split cleanly by theater-window; total Israeli dead are not separated from all other Israeli war losses in a theater-ready way.
notes: Used as context and a ceiling/floor cross-check, not as a clean phase-specific casualty source.

title: The October War and U.S. Policy
author_or_org: National Security Archive, George Washington University
pages_or_sections: intro line 307; commentary lines 375-376 on Document 21A
strengths: Provides concise, declassified-document-based opening-force counts and a dated early-loss benchmark by 9 October.
limitations: Commentary/archival compilation rather than a dedicated casualty study.
notes: Useful for lower-bound Syrian opening force and for showing how concentrated Israeli losses were by 9 October.

title: Creating a Peaceful Place of War: Revisiting the Golan Heights Border Region
geographic_scope: Golan Heights; comparative remarks on Sinai
date_scope: Post-1967 occupation context with relevance to 1973 exposure base
strengths: Provides a concrete residual Golan population figure and a qualitative reminder that Sinai was very sparsely populated.
limitations: Not a war-casualty source and not specific to daily October 1973 movements.
notes: Best denominator anchor found for resident civilians on the occupied Golan plateau.

title: Assessment of the Weapons and Tactics Used in the October 1973 Arab-Israeli War
pages_or_sections: search-result snippet (full document could not be fetched due redirect loop)
strengths: Contemporaneous intelligence estimate useful as an upper-bound denominator input.
limitations: Only the search-result snippet was accessible in this session; should be treated as an upper-bound contextual source rather than a sole basis for estimation.
notes: Used only to set the high bound on Syrian opening force exposure.

title: 134. Memorandum of Conversation
author_or_org: Office of the Historian, U.S. Department of State
strengths: Official archival reference to a Syrian civilian-casualty event during the war.
limitations: Gives civilian casualties, not deaths, and does not map cleanly onto the plateau-front denominator used here.
notes: Retained as out-of-footprint civilian context; not used in the core opening Golan estimate path.
